records.text: Bugged for years. Stop being lazy and fix it! FED UP. Friends and I have all been experiencing the same bugs, unable to play songs when clicking on them, app crashes, songs sound distorted like the speaker broke then the app crashes and repeat. We pay a lot for this app. I personally have DUO. Im extremely fed up with these same bugs happening for YEARS. I had the same issues while travelling last summer and now again theyre happening A YEAR later. At the exact same time. It’s always in the summer, when we want to hang out and blast our tunes and enjoy the weather that I start getting these bugs so PLEASE PLEASE PLEASE FIX IT. Fix. It. Please. If your app can’t handle more people using it then hire more people to fix it. Imagine hanging with your besties and you ALL REALLY want to listen to a certain song. 5 of you ALL pay for it and ALL of you experience the same issues on different phones. ** OFFLINE SPOTIFY IS GARBAGE ** Why are we evening paying for premium?! It only works properly when we have good Wi-Fi. The whole point of premium is to be able to use it without it. It’s NOT a data issue, it’s this app. Some ppl need music to live, so ffs stop cutting off my supply, you have too many resources for this kind of stuff to continuously keep happening. At this point, it’s like a slap on the face. Theres just no excuse for it anymore, it’s sloppy and lazy and they know it’s the #1 music app so they aren’t fixing it. That’s the vibe Im getting with Spotify and it’s making me want to switch to something more reliable like YouTube. Please fix it. If you can’t tel your bugged app is giving me distress then please know it is. PLEASE FIX IT!!! AHHHH!!!! Ive had this app since it began and Im really starting to feel like an idiot paying for it so I can jamsesh with friends WHEN IT NEVER WORKS!!!!!

records.text: Video update is the WORST I thought the music video / podcast update would be so good, I was so wrong. First of all the music videos take ages to load and are usually low quality. I go onto Spotify to listen to music at night and I’m met with horrible thumbnails, I don’t know how it takes over my page considering I click the NOT INTERESTED button everytime, clearly doesn’t seem to work. Even podcasts I’ve listened to once keep getting recommended on repeat and I didn’t like them that’s why I listened once, I don’t follow these podcasts and press not interested and they STILL get recommended. Just because I like some true crime girlies doesn’t mean I want horrific recommendations. I pressed follow on podcasts I am interested in and not interested on podcasts I don’t like and would honestly block if I could. What’s the point in these buttons if they don’t work? Honestly it’s ruined my entire Spotify experience not to mention it takes up more energy and storage on my phone over an update that literally IS SO ANNOYING. records.text: Easily the worst mobile music app Spotify is good if you want to pay the overpriced monthly subscription. Otherwise, you can forget about choosing what songs to listen to and listening to your own playlists/albums without spotify deciding for you what you want to listen to. Without premium, you can’t just search for and play a song you want to listen to. It will force you to listen to random reccomended songs first, then a few bunches of 1:30 of unskippable ads, and then finally if you’re lucky it will play you the song you actually searched for. Without premium, if you make a playlist and want to listen to it, spotify forces its own “recommended similar songs” into your queue which are often more commonly played instead of the songs that are actually IN THE PLAYLIST YOU CREATED. Too bad if you want to listen to an album, because spotify will instead choose other songs from other albums from the artist when you click play and then also sprinkle in some songs from entirely different artists and suddenly you’re an hour into listening and all you have heard is 20 minutes of ads, 2 album songs, 3 songs from different albums, and 6 songs from completely different artists. Also, too bad if you want to skip the songs you didn’t ask for, because there is a limit on the amount of skips you can do per hour too. Spotify free is only okay if you use desktop pc or laptop, in which these issues are non-existent.

records.text: Free tier on iPhone is deliberately worse and Spotify admits it Spotify’s free experience is NOT equal across devices, and that’s unacceptable. On Windows, macOS, and even iPadOS, free users get full on-demand listening. On iPhone and Android? You get a daily on-demand cap — once you hit it, Spotify forces you into shuffle mode, even mid-playlist. Spotify’s own community moderators openly confirmed: “The iPad has some advantages over the mobile version, just like the desktop apps.” Mobile free users are also stuck at 160kbps audio quality while desktop free users get better. This isn’t a technical limitation — it’s a deliberate design choice to pressure mobile users into paying. If you’re happy to listen free on your Mac or iPad, great. The moment you open the iPhone app, you’re a second-class user. One star until the free tier is consistent across all platforms. The double standard is insulting.

records.text: Wither Music? This kind of thing is excusable in a car type situation, given that they don’t build cars with cassette decks or CD players any more, but I don’t use it anywhere else. I much prefer a solid something in my musical listening. I’m not a vinyl lunatic or anything but I do prefer an object to all this ‘streaming’ business. Perhaps it’s mere fetishisation, yes, for music does indeed live in the ether, the air, but then there are covers, sleeves, etc to be considered. It’s certainly fair to ask ‘Where is music?’ and the answer is clearly ‘Well, quite as much on Spotify, etc as it is on any record or tape (not to mention the radio, of course)’, but still, but still. Beyond which and besides, I’d rather think my favourite artists are getting a goodly lump than some so-called tech-whizz type, to be honest. All of which said, my main gripe is actually with the present day and the future, and by using what some might see as outmoded mechanisms or whatever I can at least try to cling to some vestige of the past, the dying world, or something.

records.text: Spotify is the best Spotify is easily the most essential app on my iPhone. If you want the best way to stream music, discover new artists, and share tracks with friends, this is it.Why it’s worth the download:Unmatched Algorithm: The "Discover Weekly" and "Daily Mix" features are incredibly accurate at finding new music tailored to your exact taste.Flawless iOS Integration: The app is fast, clean, and supports seamless Handoff from your iPhone to your Mac, iPad, or HomePod.Social Sharing: It is super easy to make collaborative playlists with friends and share tracks directly to your Instagram or Snapchat stories.The AI DJ: This feature mixes your top tracks with new recommendations, complete with realistic, radio-style commentary.The only catch:The free tier is pretty limited with unskippable ads. If you want the best experience, upgrading to Premium is a must—but between the massive catalog and offline downloading, it is worth every penny.
records.author: Abby4surr

records.text: Good Spotify is easily one of the best music streaming platforms out there. The interface is clean and easy to use, and it makes discovering new music feel effortless. I love how personalized the playlists are—especially Discover Weekly and Release Radar, which always seem to match my taste perfectly. The huge library means I can find almost any song or artist I’m looking for, and the ability to create and share playlists is a big plus. The only downside is the ads on the free version, but overall, Spotify offers an amazing listening experience. If you enjoy music and want a platform that helps you explore new sounds while keeping all your favorites in one place, Spotify is definitely worth it.

records.text: You are ruining this app with AI Have been premium for years. No, I am not going to accept that ‘having too many liked songs’ is a factor as these changes have only happened in the past year or so. STOP letting AI write your code. Stop adding TikTok features - it’s a music app not a scrolling app. It was completely fine the way it used to be. I know that I am not the only user experience this slowness. Jamming it full of useless AI features that no one needs is just ruining the app experience. I have been an avid Spotify premium user for years but I am now considering switching my family to a different platform because the app is now barely functional. I doubt this review will have any effect as developers are obviously more concerned with AI-ing the app as much as possible for some sick reason instead of maintaining the reliable usable app they had before. usable_records.text: Bugged for years. Stop being lazy and fix it! FED UP. Friends and I have all been experiencing the same bugs, unable to play songs when clicking on them, app crashes, songs sound distorted like the speaker broke then the app crashes and repeat. We pay a lot for this app. I personally have DUO. Im extremely fed up with these same bugs happening for YEARS. I had the same issues while travelling last summer and now again theyre happening A YEAR later. At the exact same time. It’s always in the summer, when we want to hang out and blast our tunes and enjoy the weather that I start getting these bugs so PLEASE PLEASE PLEASE FIX IT. Fix. It. Please. If your app can’t handle more people using it then hire more people to fix it. Imagine hanging with your besties and you ALL REALLY want to listen to a certain song. 5 of you ALL pay for it and ALL of you experience the same issues on different phones. ** OFFLINE SPOTIFY IS GARBAGE ** Why are we evening paying for premium?! It only works properly when we have good Wi-Fi. The whole point of premium is to be able to use it without it. It’s NOT a data issue, it’s this app. Some ppl need music to live, so ffs stop cutting off my supply, you have too many resources for this kind of stuff to continuously keep happening. At this point, it’s like a slap on the face. Theres just no excuse for it anymore, it’s sloppy and lazy and they know it’s the #1 music app so they aren’t fixing it. That’s the vibe Im getting with Spotify and it’s making me want to switch to something more reliable like YouTube. Please fix it. If you can’t tel your bugged app is giving me distress then please know it is. PLEASE FIX IT!!! AHHHH!!!! Ive had this app since it began and Im really starting to feel like an idiot paying for it so I can jamsesh with friends WHEN IT NEVER WORKS!!!!!
usable_records.author: LaurenElizabethLolo

usable_records.text: Video update is the WORST I thought the music video / podcast update would be so good, I was so wrong. First of all the music videos take ages to load and are usually low quality. Second of all the podcast videos get recommended on your homepage with clickbait thumbnails worse than YouTube, and if you like listening to a few specific true time podcasts all they’ll recommend is horror videos and even innapropriate titled videos with creepy thumbnails. I go onto Spotify to listen to music at night and I’m met with horrible thumbnails, I don’t know how it takes over my page considering I click the NOT INTERESTED button everytime, clearly doesn’t seem to work. Even podcasts I’ve listened to once keep getting recommended on repeat and I didn’t like them that’s why I listened once, I don’t follow these podcasts and press not interested and they STILL get recommended. usable_records.text: Free tier on iPhone is deliberately worse and Spotify admits it Spotify’s free experience is NOT equal across devices, and that’s unacceptable. On Windows, macOS, and even iPadOS, free users get full on-demand listening. On iPhone and Android? You get a daily on-demand cap — once you hit it, Spotify forces you into shuffle mode, even mid-playlist. Spotify’s own community moderators openly confirmed: “The iPad has some advantages over the mobile version, just like the desktop apps.” Mobile free users are also stuck at 160kbps audio quality while desktop free users get better. This isn’t a technical limitation — it’s a deliberate design choice to pressure mobile users into paying. If you’re happy to listen free on your Mac or iPad, great. The moment you open the iPhone app, you’re a second-class user. One star until the free tier is consistent across all platforms. The double standard is insulting.

usable_records.text: Wither Music? This kind of thing is excusable in a car type situation, given that they don’t build cars with cassette decks or CD players any more, but I don’t use it anywhere else. I much prefer a solid something in my musical listening. I’m not a vinyl lunatic or anything but I do prefer an object to all this ‘streaming’ business. Perhaps it’s mere fetishisation, yes, for music does indeed live in the ether, the air, but then there are covers, sleeves, etc to be considered. It’s certainly fair to ask ‘Where is music?’ and the answer is clearly ‘Well, quite as much on Spotify, etc as it is on any record or tape (not to mention the radio, of course)’, but still, but still. Beyond which and besides, I’d rather think my favourite artists are getting a goodly lump than some so-called tech-whizz type, to be honest. All of which said, my main gripe is actually with the present day and the future, and by using what some might see as outmoded mechanisms or whatever I can at least try to cling to some vestige of the past, the dying world, or something.
usable_records.author: daisypip

usable_records.text: Spotify is the best Spotify is easily the most essential app on my iPhone. If you want the best way to stream music, discover new artists, and share tracks with friends, this is it.Why it’s worth the download:Unmatched Algorithm: The "Discover Weekly" and "Daily Mix" features are incredibly accurate at finding new music tailored to your exact taste.Flawless iOS Integration: The app is fast, clean, and supports seamless Handoff from your iPhone to your Mac, iPad, or HomePod.Social Sharing: It is super easy to make collaborative playlists with friends and share tracks directly to your Instagram or Snapchat stories.The AI DJ: This feature mixes your top tracks with new recommendations, complete with realistic, radio-style commentary.The only catch:The free tier is pretty limited with unskippable ads. If you want the best experience, upgrading to Premium is a must—but between the massive catalog and offline downloading, it is worth every penny.
usable_records.author: Abby4surr

usable_records.text: Good Spotify is easily one of the best music streaming platforms out there. The interface is clean and easy to use, and it makes discovering new music feel effortless. I love how personalized the playlists are—especially Discover Weekly and Release Radar, which always seem to match my taste perfectly. The huge library means I can find almost any song or artist I’m looking for, and the ability to create and share playlists is a big plus. The only downside is the ads on the free version, but overall, Spotify offers an amazing listening experience. If you enjoy music and want a platform that helps you explore new sounds while keeping all your favorites in one place, Spotify is definitely worth it.

usable_records.text: You are ruining this app with AI Have been premium for years. Never a single problem except for in the past year the app has gotten significantly noticeably worse, to the point that using it is infuriating . Incredibly slow and glitchy, no matter how much I clear my cache. No, I am not going to accept that ‘having too many liked songs’ is a factor as these changes have only happened in the past year or so. STOP letting AI write your code. Stop adding TikTok features - it’s a music app not a scrolling app. It was completely fine the way it used to be. I know that I am not the only user experience this slowness. Jamming it full of useless AI features that no one needs is just ruining the app experience. I have been an avid Spotify premium user for years but I am now considering switching my family to a different platform because the app is now barely functional. I doubt this review will have any effect as developers are obviously more concerned with AI-ing the app as much as possible for some sick reason instead of maintaining the reliable usable app they had before. Just insanity, not to mention the prevalence of AI music allowed on the platform now. Just feels like spitting in the face of everything human .

There are workarounds apparently that can be done on a desktop but let’s keep it simple for those on the move; subscribing customers wanting be able to save favourite albums in folders as genres. I would like a go to place where I can pick, for example, an 80’s album from a collection I have centralised in the app. I don’t want to download each individual song into its own single folder as that is not helpful. It’s in the art of the possible to design, so come on Spotify, give the people what they want and leave the other music apps even further bring. Thank you.

Checked it across multiple devices too and the same thing! This started a few months ago and seems to be a problem with every person I’ve talked to who uses Spotify as well. Just so many unnecessary features that slow down the app with no way to turn them off: concerts, merch, DJ, song DNA etc. At what point do you add so many features that it ruins the basic function of the app?
